# PlaygroundValue

**Enumeration**

The types you can save in the key-value store or send in messages to live views.

## Declaration

```swift
enum PlaygroundValue
```

## Overview

The example below stores a dictionary in a [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/playgroundsupport/playgroundvalue] instance and then stores the value in a [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/playgroundsupport/playgroundkeyvaluestore].

```swift
let myData = [
    "animal": PlaygroundValue.string("Llama"),
    "count": PlaygroundValue.integer(5)
]

PlaygroundKeyValueStore.current.keyValueStore["AnimalCountDict"] = .dictionary(myData)
```

For information about using [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/playgroundsupport/playgroundvalue] instances to send messages, see [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/playgroundsupport/messaging_between_a_playground_page_and_the_always-on_live_view#3034522].

## Representing Data

- **PlaygroundValue.boolean(_:)**: A value that represents a Boolean.
- **PlaygroundValue.data(_:)**: A value that represents raw data.
- **PlaygroundValue.date(_:)**: A value that represents a date.
- **PlaygroundValue.floatingPoint(_:)**: A value that represents a floating-point number.
- **PlaygroundValue.integer(_:)**: A value that represents an integer.
- **PlaygroundValue.string(_:)**: A value that represents a string.

## Representing Data Collections

- **PlaygroundValue.array(_:)**: A value that represents an array that contains [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/playgroundsupport/playgroundvalue] instances.
- **PlaygroundValue.dictionary(_:)**: A value that represents a mapping from strings to [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/playgroundsupport/playgroundvalue] instances.

## Data Persistence

- **PlaygroundKeyValueStore**: A data storage container you use to persist information across different sessions.
- **playgroundSharedDataDirectory**: The path to the directory containing data shared between all playgrounds in Xcode.
